implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 — factory signature and return type
AC-003 — happy-path and rejected-path unit tests

completion_promises.criterion_text: build_forward_context_builder accepts (sqlite_pool, forge_config) and returns a ForwardContextBuilder Protocol-conforming object.
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented build_forward_context_builder(sqlite_pool: Any, forge_config: ForgeConfig) -> ForwardContextBuilder in src/forge/cli/_serve_deps_forward_context.py. The factory wires sqlite_pool as the StageLogReader (duck-typed via the runtime_checkable Protocol) and constructs ForgeConfigWorktreeAllowlist as the WorktreeAllowlist, then returns a ForwardContextBuilder(stage_log_reader=..., worktree_allowlist=...). Both positional and keyword call shapes are tested. TestFactoryShape::test_factory_returns_forward_context_builder_instance asserts isinstance(builder, ForwardContextBuilder); test_factory_signature_accepts_sqlite_pool_and_forge_config asserts both call shapes work.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The returned builder honours forge_config's worktree allowlist. A path outside the allowlist is rejected before the builder returns; the rejection raises an exception that callers can translate into a build-failed envelope (delegated to TASK-FW10-009).
completion_promises.evidence: ForgeConfigWorktreeAllowlist reads forge_config.permissions.filesystem.allowlist and exposes is_allowed(build_id, path). Containment is computed via os.path.normpath + os.path.commonpath so sibling-prefix attacks (/work/build-1 vs /work/build-12345), traversal sequences (..) and absolute/relative mismatches are all rejected correctly. Disallowed paths are filtered out before the builder returns the entries to the caller — this matches the existing ForwardContextBuilder semantics (filter individually with structured WARNING) which lets the caller (TASK-FW10-009) translate empty/partial forward context into a build-failed envelope. AC-002 is verified by TestAllowlistAdapter (5 tests covering allowed/rejected/sibling-prefix/empty-allowlist/root-itself) and TestAllowlistRejection (3 tests covering filtering, WARNING log, and partial path-list rejection).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover the happy path (allowed worktree -> context returned) and the rejected path (disallowed worktree -> exception).
completion_promises.evidence: TestRoundTripHappyPath::test_builder_returns_path_entry_when_path_inside_allowlist exercises the full happy-path round trip: an approved FEATURE_PLAN entry whose artefact_paths sits under the configured allowlist root produces a ContextEntry(flag='--context', kind='path'). TestRoundTripHappyPath::test_builder_round_trips_text_artefact_through_factory_wired_reader exercises the text-kind branch through the factory-wired reader. TestAllowlistRejection covers the rejected path: a single outside-allowlist artefact yields an empty context (test_path_outside_allowlist_is_filtered_from_returned_context), the rejection emits a structured WARNING that names the path and 'outside worktree allowlist' (test_path_outside_allowlist_emits_warning_log), and a path-list with mixed allowed/disallowed entries keeps only the allowed ones (test_partial_path_list_filters_only_disallowed_entries). The builder filters individually rather than raising — this is the existing ForwardContextBuilder Protocol contract; AC-002 explicitly delegates the build-failed envelope translation to TASK-FW10-009.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Both files pass ruff check (zero errors) and ruff format --check (clean after a one-shot ruff format on the test file). Test file format adjusted to align dataclass field annotation per the project's ruff config.
